reasoning: C has the most genuinely distinct set: it explicitly names and breaks a different governing assumption per version, and its Silent Hour Exchange (cross-firm synchronized quiet as enforced infrastructure that only works at scale) and Omerta Underwriter (secrecy reinsured like catastrophe risk, priced in basis points) are the freshest, least-cliche ideas in the whole field. B is close and best-crafted prose, but its Negative Easement and Quiet Index both land in the same property/asset conceptual space, costing it internal spread. A is solidly diverse (Blank Sponsor, Absence Insurance, Quiet Credits, Unsaid Archive, Silence Exchange) but each entry is compressed to a single paragraph with less development, and its silence-credits idea overlaps the financialization space that B and C both render more vividly.
